parked_wiki_only.quote: Lord Roger Reyne, Ellyn's older brother, laughed when he read Tywin's edicts and advised his friends and vassals to do nothing.
parked_wiki_only.note: Rebel side; not found named directly in book text searched (asos/affc), only in wiki-sourced node Origins.

parked_wiki_only.quote: Lord Walderan Tarbeck hoped to convince Tytos to rescind Tywin's edicts and traveled to Casterly Rock, but was instead imprisoned by Tywin.
parked_wiki_only.note: See flags — asos-jaime-06.md:91 book text attributes an imprisonment episode of 'Lord Tarbeck' to Tytos Lannister, not Tywin, conflicting with this wiki claim.

parked_wiki_only.quote: In return, his wife Ellyn seized three Lannisters – two Lannisters of Lannisport, and Stafford Lannister, whose sister Joanna was betrothed to Tywin – and threatened them harm unless her husband was returned.
parked_wiki_only.note: Taken hostage by Ellyn Tarbeck; not found named directly in the book chapters searched.

parked_wiki_only.quote: Mathos sailed for Sisterton with a hundred warships. Although he never returned from war, his sons and descendants continued the fight against the northmen for a thousand years.
parked_wiki_only.note: Vale/Arryn side; ancient TWOIAF-era history, no book-chapter text available in sources/chapters (no TWOIAF source cached, only novels/D&E/F&B).

parked_wiki_only.quote: The Starks attacked Gulltown and, unable to overcome its walls, burned hundreds of ships in the city's harbor.
parked_wiki_only.note: Northmen side; same TWOIAF-only sourcing caveat as R4-49.

no_node.name: Prince Aegon (son of Aenys I, "Aegon the Uncrowned")
no_node.note: rebelled against Maegor prior to the events of this uprising node (his own rebellion/death is a preceding event, not part of the Jaehaerys uprising itself); did not search for a node since he is out of scope for this event's role edges

no_node.quote: The man was a criminal, if that gives you any solace.
no_node.note: The unnamed tattooed criminal substituted and actually beheaded in Davos's place — the deception's true physical victim/patient. No node exists for him.
